channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Zimbabwe takes helm of African Registrars General Bureau
item.description: Rutendo Nyeve ZIMBABWE has assumed the chairmanship of the Bureau of African Registrars General, a milestone that positions the country at the forefront of efforts to strengthen civil... (via H-metro) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: H-metro

item.title: Zimbabwe rejects lithium miners’ request to delay export ban
item.description: Bloomberg Zimbabwe won’t defer a ban on exports of lithium concentrate that’s scheduled to come into effect in January, Mines Minister Polite Kambamura said, rejecting a request by the industry to postpone the moratorium until at least March. “We are still sticking with January 1,” Kambamura told reporters on Friday at a mine owned by [...] The post Zimbabwe rejects lithium miners’ request to delay export ban appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: ZDF benchmarks ZPCS commercial ventures to boost self-sustaining enterprises
item.description: Sikhumbuzo Moyo THE Zimbabwe Defence Forces (ZDF) on Wednesday conducted a benchmarking visit to the Zimbabwe Prisons and Correctional Service (ZPCS) to learn from the correctional service’s industrialisation... (via H-metro) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: H-metro

item.title: Rebuilding Zimbabwe’s Credit Economy: Why a Trusted National Digital Identity System Is Critical for Financial Inclusion, Lending and Economic Recovery
item.description: Zimbabwe’s journey towards economic reconstruction requires more than currency reforms, banking regulation and capital injection; it requires rebuilding the institutional foundations upon which a modern financial economy operates. At the centre of every successful financial system is trust, trust that individuals can be accurately identified, that businesses can demonstrate their economic activity, that lenders can [...] The post Rebuilding Zimbabwe’s Credit Economy: Why a Trusted National Digital Identity System Is Critical for Financial Inclusion, Lending and Economic Recovery appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Government says no evidence links COVID-19 vaccines to children’s developmental disorders @nehandaradio
item.description: The Ministry of Health says Zimbabwe has not recorded any evidence linking COVID-19 vaccines to developmental or growth disorders in children, following concerns raised by some parents and legislators in Parliament. The post Government says no evidence links COVID-19 vaccines to children’s developmental disorders appeared first on Nehanda Radio. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Nehanda Radio

item.title: Saint Augustine’s College launches the Advanced Design Colleges (ADC) Laboratory

item.dc:creator: H-metro

item.title: Air Zimbabwe’s London-bound airbus arrives at RGM
item.description: Freeman Razemba Senior Reporter The Airbus A330-300 that will operate Air Zimbabwe’s revived direct Harare-London service arrived at Robert Gabriel Mugabe International Airport today, marking a major milestone ahead of... (via H-metro) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: H-metro

item.title: Air Zimbabwe Receives Airbus A330 Ahead of Landmark Return to London After 14 Years
item.description: HARARE – Air Zimbabwe has taken delivery of an Airbus A330-300, marking a significant milestone in the national carrier’s long-haul recovery strategy as it prepares to relaunch direct flights between Harare and London for the first time in 14 years. The wide-body aircraft, secured under a wet-lease arrangement, arrived at Robert Gabriel Mugabe International Airport [...] The post Air Zimbabwe Receives Airbus A330 Ahead of Landmark Return to London After 14 Years appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Combating drug abuse trafficking: Zimbabwe to learn from Ghana
item.description: By Edward Dankwah Accra, July 16, GNA - Mr Kufa E. Chinoza, the Ambassador of Zimbabwe to Ghana. has paid a courtesv call in Accra, on Maior General Maxwell Obuba Mantey, the Director-General of the Narcotics Control Commission (NACOC). The visit was to formally brief the Commission’s management on a proposed benchmarking mission by Zimbabwe’s soon-to-be establishec E Drua and Substance Abuse Control and Enforcement Agency. E Geographic Reference A statement issued to the Ghana News Agency by the NACOC, said the visit formed part of Zimbabwe’s preparations to operationalise its new national anti-drug agency,which was expected to assume the lead role in coordinating the country’s response to drug trafficking, substance abuse, prevention treatment and rehabilitation once the enabling egislation comes into force. Speaking during the meeting, Mr Chinoza explained that Zimbabwe’s Transition Task Team intended to undertake a benchmarking exercise in selected African countries with established and successful drug control institutions, with Ghana being one of its key destinations. He noted that the visit sought to study Ghana’s institutional framework and operational strategies that have earned NACOC continenta recognition as one of Africa’s leading drug control agencies. E Geographic Reference The benchmarking mission will enable Zimbabwe to draw practical lessons from Ghana’s experience in areas including institutional design, governance systems, intelligence-led enforcement, operational models, rehabilitation programmes, border management, international cooperation mechanisms and inter-agency coordination,’ he added. He said the exercise would lay the foundation for long-term collaboration between the two countries by creating opportunities for technica cooperation, intelligence sharing, capacity building and enhanced law enforcement partnerships in combating transnationa organised crime and illicit drug trafficking. E Drug Laws & Policy Mr Chinoza revealed that Ghana’s approach to narcotics control recently received continenta recognition during the 13th Africa Security Symposium (ASEC 2026). Maior General Mantey in his remarks, expressed appreciation to the Government of Zimbabwe for selectina Ghana as one of the countries to benchmark its emerging anti-drug institution. He reaffirmed NACOC’s commitment to supporting African countries in strengthening their drug control systems through knowledge sharing and collaboration. The Director-General assured the ambassador’s delegation of the Commission’s readiness to share its experiences, operational approaches and best practices in narcotics control. He acknowledged that loaistics continued tc pose a challenge for many drug law enforcement agencies across the continent, however, sustained government commitment and institutional reforms had enabled NACOC to make significant strides in both drug supply reduction and drug demand reduction. While we enforce the law rigorously against drug traffickers, we are equally committed to educating especially our young people to stay away from drugs, he added. Major General Mantey stressed that an effective national drug control strategy must strike a balance between robust law enforcement and sustained public education, prevention, treatment and rehabilitation interventions. The meeting reaffirmed the growing cooperation between Ghana and Zimbabwe in addressing the evolving challenges of illicit drug trafficking and substance abuse, underscoring the importance of regional collaboration in building resilient drug control institutions across Africa. The proposed benchmarking visit is expected to deepen bilateral relations between the two countries while providing Zimbabwe with valuable insights into Ghana’s integrated and people-centred approach to narcotics control. It also reflects the increasing recognition of NACOC’s operational model as a reference point for strengthening drug control qovernance and inter-agency coordination across the African continent. GNA (via The Sunday News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Sunday News

item.title: Air Zimbabwe Officially Launches Long-Awaited Direct Harare-London Route Today
item.description: Air Zimbabwe Relaunches Harare-London Route in Major International Comeback Zimbabwe’s national airline is taking a major step towards rebuilding its international network as it unveils its long-awaited London connection at Robert Gabriel Mugabe International Airport today. The ceremony comes ahead of the first Harare-London Gatwick flight, which is scheduled to take off on Wednesday, bringing [...] The post Air Zimbabwe Officially Launches Long-Awaited Direct Harare-London Route Today appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: Bangladesh claim T20I consolation after Zimbabwe’s Test-ODI double @3menonaboat
item.description:
The victory gave the tourists a consolation 2-1 T20I series win after losing both the Test and ODI contests.

The post Bangladesh claim T20I consolation after Zimbabwe’s Test-ODI double first appeared on Three Men On a Boat.

(via Three Men On Boat) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Three Men On Boat

item.title: Raza becomes Zimbabwe’s most capped international cricketer @3menonaboat @ZimCricketv
item.description:
Zimbabwe Cricket (ZC) has hailed Sikandar Raza after he became the country’s most capped international cricketer

The post Raza becomes Zimbabwe’s most capped international cricketer first appeared on Three Men On a Boat.

(via Three Men On Boat) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Three Men On Boat

item.title: Caledonia buoyed by improvement in access to higher-grade ore
item.description: Gold miner Caledonia Mining produced 17 360 oz of gold from its Blanket mine, in Zimbabwe, in the quarter ended June 30, an 18% increase from the 14 767 oz produced in the previous quarter, reflecting improving access to higher-grade mining areas. Grades delivered to the plant have improved steadily since January, further reflecting progress in restoring access to higher-grade ore, the company points out. (via Australasia Global Mining News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Australasia Global Mining News

item.title: Vaginal tightening herbs and guchu disaster: Men die during lula lula, Epworth woman dies while enlarging her genital ‘twins’
item.description: The Hidden Dangers: Unregulated Herbal Products and the Rising Toll on Health in Zimbabwe Zimbabwe is grappling with a silent health crisis as the unregulated market for traditional herbal products, particularly those marketed for intimate health and sexual enhancement, continues to claim lives and jeopardise public well-being. From vaginal tightening concoctions linked to cervical cancer [...] The post Vaginal tightening herbs and guchu disaster: Men die during lula lula, Epworth woman dies while enlarging her genital ‘twins’ first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News
